sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 10:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 10:1
items.html: It causes its branches in the Guf, which are ZON in the vessel of Keter that receive its illumination, not to be able to bestow that great light that they receive from it to the lower ones from Keter of the Guf. This is so because the posterior of the light of Keter detain and cease its illumination so as not to expand from the vessel of Keter of the Guf downward. (Items 4, 5)
